questionText: The curve has equation $y = \sin x$ for $0 \le x \le 2\pi$, where $x$ is in radians. Find the exact coordinates of the points on the curve at which the gradient equals $\tfrac{1}{2}$.

steps.description: The gradient is the derivative; work in radians
steps.explanation: We want where the gradient equals $\tfrac{1}{2}$, so we differentiate and set the derivative equal to $\tfrac{1}{2}$. Trig derivatives require $x$ in radians.

steps.description: Write the standard derivative of sin x
steps.explanation: The derivative of $\sin x$ is the standard result $\cos x$.
This gives the gradient of the curve at any value of $x$.
This equation locates every point on the curve with the required gradient.

steps.description: Find the first solution in the interval
steps.explanation: In the first quadrant $\cos\tfrac{\pi}{3}=\tfrac{1}{2}$, giving the principal solution.

steps.description: Find the second solution in the interval
steps.explanation: Cosine is also positive in the fourth quadrant; the symmetry $\cos(2\pi-\theta)=\cos\theta$ gives the second solution within $0 \le x \le 2\pi$.
In the fourth quadrant sine is negative, giving $-\tfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$.
Both points lie in the interval and both have gradient $\tfrac{1}{2}$.
Both evaluate to a gradient of $\tfrac{1}{2}$, confirming the answers.
